description: Wiki page type template system reverse-engineered from SuperMemory docs (supermemory.ai/docs). Maps Mintlify patterns to Fumadocs equivalents for Kijko WikiAgent generation.

landing_overview.name: Landing / Overview
landing_overview.description: Product or section entry point. Establishes value prop, shows capabilities at a glance, directs to key journeys.

sections.name: Hero
sections.content: H1 title + 1-2 sentence value proposition + optional subtitle badge

sections.name: At a Glance
sections.content: Key capabilities overview. Variant determines layout.

variant_notes.card_grid: 2-3 column CardGroup with icons, titles, and one-line descriptions. Used for section overviews with many sub-pages.
variant_notes.bullet_list: Bullet list with inline links to key pages. Used when overview has fewer items.
variant_notes.prose_subsections: H4 subsections with prose + images (e.g., Ingestion, Memory API, User Profiles). Used for the main intro page.

sections.name: Product Visualization
sections.content: Dark-themed screenshot, architecture diagram, or interactive demo embed

sections.name: How It Works (Brief)
sections.content: 3-5 bullet summary of core mechanism or numbered steps

sections.name: Use Cases / Key Features
sections.content: CardGroup or bullet list of primary use cases with brief descriptions

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: 2-column CardGroup linking to Quickstart and key concept pages

quickstart_tutorial.name: Quickstart / Tutorial
quickstart_tutorial.description: Hands-on onboarding guide. Gets reader from zero to working in minimal steps.

sections.name: Title + Subtitle
sections.content: H1 + one-line goal statement ('Make your first API call')

sections.name: Prerequisites
sections.content: Warning/Info callout listing requirements (API key, runtime version, etc.). Some quickstarts embed prerequisites inline instead of a dedicated section.

sections.name: Numbered Steps
sections.content: Step-by-step instructions with code blocks.

variant_notes.steps_component: Fumadocs Steps component with H2 per step. Each step: brief explanation + code block with language tabs.
variant_notes.bold_inline: Bold inline step numbers ('**Step 1.** Do X') within prose paragraphs. Less formal, used in shorter quickstarts.
variant_notes.h3_numbered: H3 headings with numbered step titles. Middle ground between Steps component and bold inline.

sections.name: Dashboard Screenshot
sections.content: Dark-themed screenshot showing relevant UI (e.g., API key creation)

sections.name: Verification / What Happens
sections.content: Expected output or behavior description. Bullet list of what the system does automatically.

sections.name: Pro Tip
sections.content: Tip or Note callout with advanced guidance

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: 2-column CardGroup or text link list. CardGroup preferred but text links ('Learn more about User Profiles and Search') are acceptable for shorter quickstarts.

concept_explainer.name: Concept Explainer
concept_explainer.description: Deep conceptual article. Builds mental model progressively from analogy to mechanism to implications.

sections.name: Title + Context
sections.content: H1 + 2-3 sentence framing that establishes why this concept matters

sections.name: Mental Model / Analogy
sections.content: Comparison of two or more concepts to build intuition.

variant_notes.card_group: CardGroup comparing two concepts with icons and bullet traits (e.g., Documents vs Memories).
variant_notes.dual_heading: Two adjacent H2 blocks (e.g., '## Traditional Systems' / '## Supermemory') with bullet lists under each, rendering as a side-by-side comparison.
variant_notes.prose_analogy: Prose paragraph using a real-world analogy to introduce the concept. Used when no direct comparison exists.

sections.name: Architecture Visualization
sections.content: Dark-themed diagram showing internal architecture or data flow

sections.name: How It Works (Deep)
sections.content: H2 sections breaking down the mechanism. Code blocks showing data structures. Tables for pipeline stages.

sections.name: Comparison / Trade-offs
sections.content: Side-by-side comparison blocks with 'When to Use Each' guidance.

variant_notes.table: Comparison table with Aspect/Approach A/Approach B columns.
variant_notes.dual_heading: Two adjacent H2 blocks showing different approaches with bullet lists.
variant_notes.tabbed_scenario: 3+ tab comparison (e.g., 'The Scenario' / 'RAG Approach' / 'Memory Approach') showing the same problem solved different ways.

sections.name: Real-World Examples
sections.content: Concrete use case code blocks showing the concept in action

sections.name: The Bottom Line
sections.content: Callout with 3-5 bullet summary of key takeaways

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: 2-column CardGroup to related concepts and practical guides

content_rules.code_blocks: show data structures, not full implementations

integration_guide.name: Integration Guide
integration_guide.description: Step-by-step guide for integrating with a specific framework, SDK, or platform.

sections.name: Title + Logo/Badge
sections.content: H1 with integration name + product image/logo + one-line integration description

sections.name: External Link Callout
sections.content: Info callout linking to the integration partner's docs

sections.name: Overview / What It Does
sections.content: Brief paragraph + bullet list of capabilities

sections.name: Get API Key
sections.content: Tabbed code or link to console for API key acquisition

sections.name: Installation
sections.content: Code block with package install command (npm/pip/etc.)

sections.name: Configuration
sections.content: Environment variables, config file examples, settings table

sections.name: Usage / How It Works
sections.content: Numbered steps or code walkthrough showing core integration

sections.name: What Gets Synced / Capabilities Table
sections.content: Table showing Category | Keywords | Example format

sections.name: API Reference / Functions
sections.content: Function signatures with TypeScript/Python type annotations, parameter tables, and return type documentation. More detailed than a simple 'commands' list.

sections.name: Implementation Choice
sections.content: Two-approach structure (e.g., 'withSupermemory wrapper' vs 'Function calling tools'). CardGroup offering parallel tracks.

sections.name: Modes Comparison
sections.content: Table comparing different modes or configurations (e.g., memory modes table).

sections.name: Advanced Configuration
sections.content: Optional deeper config (custom OAuth, self-hosted, etc.)

sections.name: Advanced Details (Accordion)
sections.content: Expandable Accordion sections for Parameter Details, Error Handling, Advanced Configuration.

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: 2-column CardGroup to related integrations and guides

content_rules.hero_can_include_banner: Hero section can include a full-width banner image, not just an inline logo

connector_guide.name: Connector Guide
connector_guide.description: Setup guide for a data source connector. Covers auth, config, sync, and troubleshooting.

sections.name: Title + Subtitle
sections.content: H1 with connector name + one-line description

sections.name: Plan Requirement
sections.content: Warning callout if specific plan is required

sections.name: Quick Setup
sections.content: 3-4 steps: Create connection → Handle OAuth → Configure resources. Tabbed code (TS/Python/cURL).

sections.name: Supported Document Types
sections.content: File extension list, indexing behavior, content type mapping

sections.name: Sync / Webhooks
sections.content: How automatic sync works, webhook setup, incremental sync

sections.name: Connection Management
sections.content: CRUD operations with code examples.

sub_pattern_notes.list_connections: List all connections with code example and response schema.
sub_pattern_notes.delete_connection: Delete a connection with confirmation pattern.
sub_pattern_notes.manual_sync: Trigger manual sync with code example.

sections.name: Webhook Details
sections.content: How It Works diagram, Webhook Security details, incremental sync behavior.

sections.name: Pipeline Diagram
sections.content: Visual flow diagram showing the connector pipeline (e.g., External Document -> Webhook -> Processing -> Index).

sections.name: Advanced Configuration
sections.content: Custom OAuth, enterprise setup, self-hosted options

sections.name: Advanced Details (Accordion)
sections.content: Expandable Accordion sections for Parameter Details, Error Handling, Advanced Configuration.

sections.name: Troubleshooting
sections.content: Common issues and fixes

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: CardGroup linking to other connectors and troubleshooting

api_reference.name: API Reference
api_reference.description: Auto-generated endpoint documentation from OpenAPI spec. Shows method, path, params, request/response.

api_reference_layout.left_content: Parameter documentation with type annotations, 'Show child attributes' toggle for nested types
api_reference_layout.right_content: Dark code example block (JavaScript fetch examples)

sections.name: Endpoint Header
sections.content: HTTP method badge (POST=blue, GET=green, DELETE=red) + path + operation summary

sections.name: Description
sections.content: Multi-line endpoint description with context

sections.name: Authentication
sections.content: Required auth scheme (Bearer token, API key)

sections.name: Parameters
sections.content: Path params, query params with types, descriptions, defaults. Includes 'Show child attributes' toggle for nested types.

sections.name: Request Body
sections.content: JSON schema with required/optional fields, nested types, validation rules

sections.name: Response
sections.content: Tabbed by status code (200/201, 400, 401, 404, 500) with response schemas

sections.name: Code Examples
sections.content: Single JavaScript fetch example displayed in the right dark panel. Note: tabbed SDK examples (TS/Python/cURL) are only used in non-API-reference pages.

sections.name: Try It (Interactive)
sections.content: Try It button that opens interactive playground (not an embedded widget)

api_reference.generation_note: API reference pages are auto-generated from OpenAPI specs via fumadocs-openapi. The wiki agent should generate the OpenAPI spec, not the rendered pages. Layout handled by fumadocs-openapi, not hand-built.

cookbook_recipe.name: Cookbook / Recipe
cookbook_recipe.description: Complete implementation walkthrough. Builds a working feature from scratch with full code.

sections.name: Title + Value Hook
sections.content: H1 + paragraph explaining what you'll build and what capabilities it has

sections.name: Prerequisites
sections.content: Bullet list: API key, runtime versions, packages. Warning callout for security.

sections.name: Implementation Choice
sections.content: CardGroup offering parallel tracks (e.g., Python + FastAPI vs TypeScript + Next.js)

sections.name: Project Setup
sections.content: Environment config (.env), package install, project structure

sections.name: Step-by-Step Implementation
sections.content: H2 per major step (e.g., '## Step 1: Project Setup'), H3 per sub-step (e.g., '### Import Dependencies', '### Initialize Client', '### Define Schema'). Pattern for each step: explanation paragraph -> code block with filename header -> bridge paragraph connecting to next step. Sub-steps (H3) break down complex steps into digestible pieces.

sections.name: Complete Code
sections.content: Accordion with full implementation for reference

sections.name: Testing / Verification
sections.content: Test conversation examples, expected outputs, data inspection scripts

sections.name: Troubleshooting
sections.content: Common issues Q&A format

sections.name: Customization / Extensions
sections.content: Ideas for extending the implementation

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: CardGroup to related recipes and API reference

content_rules.pacing: short prereq → medium setup → long implementation → short testing

reference_spec.name: Reference / Specification
reference_spec.description: Formal reference documentation for CLI, config, metrics, or architecture.

sections.name: Title + Purpose
sections.content: H1 + brief description of what this reference covers

sections.name: Overview
sections.content: High-level summary of the component/feature

sections.name: Commands / Fields / Metrics
sections.content: Structured table or definition list. Columns: Name, Type, Description, Default.

sections.name: Usage Examples
sections.content: Code blocks showing common usage patterns

sections.name: Configuration
sections.content: Config file examples, environment variables

sections.name: Architecture Details
sections.content: Diagrams, component relationships, data flow

sections.name: Related References
sections.content: CardGroup to related specs and guides

migration_guide.name: Migration Guide
migration_guide.description: Guides migration from a competing product. Emphasizes before/after and step-by-step.

sections.name: Title + Context
sections.content: H1 'Migrating from X to Y' + brief rationale

sections.name: Quick Migration
sections.content: All-in-one migration script for users who want to skip the detailed walkthrough. Placed before the step-by-step instructions.

sections.name: API Mapping
sections.content: Side-by-side tabbed code blocks showing equivalent operations in old vs new SDK. Tabs labeled with product names (e.g., 'Mem0' / 'Supermemory').

sections.name: Concept Mapping
sections.content: Before/after code blocks showing equivalent operations

sections.name: Migration Steps
sections.content: Numbered steps for the migration process

sections.name: Common Gotchas
sections.content: Warning callouts for migration pitfalls

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: CardGroup to quickstart and concepts

feature_guide.name: Feature / Operations Guide
feature_guide.description: SDK feature walkthrough with parameter tables, code examples, and accordion-wrapped advanced content. Covers authentication, core API operations, and feature-specific guides.

sections.name: Title + Description
sections.content: H1 title + one-line description of the feature/operation

sections.name: Quick Start Code
sections.content: Tabbed code block (TypeScript / Python / cURL) showing the most common usage in minimal lines

sections.name: Parameters Table
sections.content: TypeTable with columns: Name, Type, Default, Description. Marks required params. Includes 'Show child attributes' for nested objects.

sections.name: Operational Sections
sections.content: Multiple H2 sections, each covering one operation or use case. Pattern: explanation paragraph -> code block with language tabs -> expected output or behavior note.

sections.name: Advanced Details (Accordion)
sections.content: Expandable Accordion sections for: Error Handling, Batch Upload, Delete Content, Response Schema, Filter Types, Parameter Details. Each accordion contains code examples and tables.

sections.name: Next Steps
sections.content: 2-column CardGroup linking to related features, API reference, and concept pages

every_page_has: Right-rail 'On this page' table of contents
Breadcrumb navigation in header (small gray text above H1)
'Was this page helpful?' Yes/No feedback widget at footer
Previous / Next page navigation with arrows
AI Assistant widget (Ctrl+I) with pre-filled suggestion prompts
Logo with light/dark variants in header

navigation.header: 60px sticky header with logo, search (Cmd+K), 'Ask AI' button, CTA, and settings gear. See design_tokens.header.
navigation.section_tabs: Horizontal tab bar below header with icon+text tabs for major sections. Active tab has dark underline. See design_tokens.section_tab_bar.
navigation.sidebar: Collapsible tree with section grouping and icons. Not visible on all pages (hidden on changelog, memorybench overview). Scrolls independently.
navigation.toc: Right-rail 'On this page' with H2/H3 links. Active item highlighted in blue (link_color).
navigation.footer: Previous / Next page navigation with arrows + social icons (GitHub, LinkedIn, X) + 'Powered by' branding
navigation.search: Cmd+K modal with full-text + AI-powered search ('Ask AI' button with sparkle icon)

universal_patterns.content_flow: mental_model → definitions → mechanisms → examples → action → next_steps
visual_hierarchy: No H4+ in most pages — kept flat

mintlify_to_fumadocs.Mermaid/SequenceDiagram: MermaidDiagram (custom component using rehype-mermaid plugin or inline <Mermaid> component). Renders flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and pipeline flows.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.HTTPMethodBadge: MethodBadge (custom component). Colors: POST=#2563eb blue, GET=#16a34a green, PUT=#d97706 amber, DELETE=#dc2626 red. Uppercase, ~11-12px, weight 600.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.ShowChildAttributes: ExpandableType (custom component). Toggle to reveal nested schema properties. Used in API reference parameter docs.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.ComingSoonCard: DisabledCard (custom component). Grayed-out card with 'Coming Soon' badge. No click handler. Used for upcoming features/recipes.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.PlatformTabs: Tabs (fumadocs-ui/components/tabs) with items={['macOS', 'Linux', 'Windows']}. Document as a Tabs pattern variant for OS-specific instructions.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.PlanCallout: Callout (fumadocs-ui/components/callout) with type='warn' and a plan badge (e.g., 'Pro Plan Required'). Extend to all templates, not just connectors.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.PricingTable: PricingTable (custom component). Distinct from standard TypeTable. Shows tier name, price, features, and CTA button per column.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.StatusCodeTabs: Tabs (fumadocs-ui/components/tabs) with items={['200', '400', '401', '500']}. Each tab shows the response schema for that status code.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.ExternalLinkCard: Card (fumadocs-ui/components/card) with external URL badge (northeast arrow icon, ~12px). href opens in new tab.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.HeroImage: ImageZoom (fumadocs-ui/components/image-zoom) with hero positioning CSS (full-width, dark-themed product screenshot).
mintlify_to_fumadocs.LiveDemoBadge: LiveDemoBadge (custom inline CTA component). Small styled link badge with 'Try Live Demo' text and external link icon.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.ResponseHeadersTable: TypeTable (fumadocs-ui/components/type-table) with documented pattern for response headers (Name, Value, Description columns).
mintlify_to_fumadocs.HorizontalRule: Standard markdown --- or * * * rendered as a thin #e5e7eb line. Used between major H2 sections in ~25 pages.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.FeedbackWidget: Custom 'Was this page helpful?' component with Yes/No pill buttons. Positioned after last content section, before footer nav.
mintlify_to_fumadocs.CopyPageButton: Custom ghost button with clipboard icon + 'Copy page' text. Positioned top-right of content area, aligned with H1.

content_length_targets.description: Per-page-type word count targets. Agent must check word count after generation.

api_reference.note: Generated from OpenAPI spec via fumadocs-openapi. No manual word count target.

cross_link_strategy.description: Two-pass cross-linking to ensure all pages are properly linked.

pass_1_generation.description: During page generation, insert placeholder links for pages that may not exist yet.
pass_1_generation.example: See [LINK:concepts/graph-memory] for details on how graph memory works.
rules: Every page must have at least 2 inline links to related concept pages
Every page must end with 2+ Next Steps cards

pass_2_resolution.description: After all pages are generated, resolve placeholders to actual paths.
steps: Scan all generated pages for [LINK:slug] placeholders
Resolve each placeholder to an actual file path
Add missing Next Steps cards where pages have fewer than 2 outgoing links

degradation_rules.description: Fallback rules when MCP tools are unavailable. Ensures generation continues with reduced fidelity.

codegraph_unavailable.fallback: Skip CodeGraph semantic analysis. Use file-tree scanning and grep-based symbol resolution instead.
codegraph_unavailable.impact: Callers/callees analysis missing. Generate based on import/export statements only.

notebooklm_unavailable.fallback: Use Mermaid code blocks instead of NotebookLM infographics. Generate text-based architecture diagrams.
notebooklm_unavailable.impact: No polished infographic images. Mermaid diagrams are functional but less visually refined.

repomix_unavailable.fallback: Use raw file reads with 50K token cap. Prioritize entry-point files, config, and READMEs.
repomix_unavailable.impact: May miss some files in large repos. Focus on the most important files.

llm_failure.fallback: Generate a skeleton page using the page type template with placeholder content. Mark as draft.
llm_failure.impact: Page exists in navigation but content is incomplete. Requires manual review.
